english: γziṅs-čúń (gzings chung) a small vessel Cs.; γziṅs-pa (gzings pa) ship-master, captain.

γzim-pa (gzim pa), also with mnal (mnal), resp. for gñíd-log-pa (gnyid log pa), 1. to fall asleep Dzl. — 2. to sleep, rgyál-po γzim-pa-las (rgyal po gzim pa las) whilst the king was sleeping Glr. — 3. to expire, to die Tar. 4, 20. — Comp. *zím-kyon* (*zim kyon*) W., resp. for rkyón-rtse (rkyon rtse), candle, lamp. — γzim-ḱaṅ (gzim khang) 1. sleeping-room. 2. dwelling, habitation. — γzim-ḱebs (gzim khebs) quilt. — γzim-ḱom (gzim khom) cloak-bag, portmanteau. — γzim-ḱri (gzim khri) bedstead. — γzim-gur (gzim gur) sleeping-tent. — *zim-gúg* (*zim gug*) C. porter, door-keeper. — γzim-ča (gzim cha) bedding, bed-clothes Gyatch. — *zim-tiṅ, zim-ter* (*zim ting, zim ter*) W. lamp. — *zim-tiṅ* (*zim ting*) -btiṅ (-bting) Sik. bedstead? — γzim-ťúl (gzim thul) sheep-skins for night-quarters. — γzim-dpon (gzim dpon) body-servant, valet-de-chambre, = sku-mdún-pa (sku mdun pa); γzim-ṕrúg (gzim phrug) his subordinate servants or pages. — γzim-mál (gzim mal) bed-linen. — γzim-yól (gzim yol) bed-curtain.
γzim-γzim (gzim gzim) W., C., *mig zim-zim ča dug* (*mig zim zim cha dug*) W. the eyes are dazzled, by a glaring light.

γzir-ba (gzir ba) (acc. to Cs. fut. of _otsir-ba ('tsir ba), certainly related to it, but chiefly used in an intellectual sense), to be pressed, harassed, troubled, to suffer, to be pressed by necessity, to suffer from hunger, disease etc. B., C. — Sch. also gzi γzir-ba (gzi gzir ba) a stinging pain in the chest.
C. = spu-ziṅ byéd-pa (spu zing byed pa).
a lever, bar; = gsó-mo (gso mo) Cs.; γzu-rṅás (gzu rngas) a prop Cs.

Cs.: 1. straight, right. — 2. upright, honest. Lexx.: ťugs γzú-bo (thugs gzu bo), from which it appears to be a word of civility, but little known. Sch. has besides: γzu-dpáṅ (gzu dpang), which he renders by 'witness, mediator'.

Lexx. = bab-čol (bab col) and सहसा, hence signifying rashness, impetuosity, so Cs., and therefore γzu-lúm-čan (gzu lum can) inconsiderate; γzu-lúm byéd-pa (gzu lum byed pa) to act rashly; Sch. also: disobedience, pride, haughtiness.
1. v. zug (zug). — 2. top, lai (la'i) of a mountain-pass Mil.
hurting, giving pain, żes γzúg-ge-ba żús-nas (zhes gzug ge ba zhus nas) as she spoke words that gave so much pain Mil. nt.
to be able to bear, to sustain, v. sub tsog (tsog).

Ssk. रूप, 1. figure, form, shape, ṕyi-rol-gyi γzúgs-rnams (phyi rol gyi gzugs rnams) the forms of the sensible world, the impressions that are made on the eye Wdn.; mig-gis γzúgs-rnams mtoṅ (mig gis gzugs rnams mthong) the forms (of things) are seen with the eyes; ráb-tu-byuṅ-bai γzugs (rab tu byung ba'i gzugs) the (painted) figure of a priest Glr.; sim. klui γzugs gsér-las byás-pa (klu'i gzugs gser las byas pa) Tar.; lus-γzúgs (lus gzugs) shape of body, stature, frq.; srín-moi γzúgs-su byéd-pa (srin mo'i gzugs su byed pa) to transform one's self into a Rākshasi Glr.; rnál-_obyor-pai γzugs byéd-pa (rnal 'byor pa'i gzugs byed pa) to assume the outward appearance of a hermit Mil.; in metaphysics: form, body, as one of the five Skandhas, v. ṕuṅ-po (phung po). — 2. resp. sku-γzúgs (sku gzugs), W. *zúg-po* (*zug po*) = lus (lus), body, *zúg-po ťú-če* (*zug po thu che*) W. to wash the body, to bathe; *zúg-po záṅ-wa mi dug, mi-dé-wa dug* (*zug po zang wa mi dug, mi de wa dug*) C., *dé-mo mi dug* (*de mo mi dug*) W. euphem. for: she has just her courses. — γzugs-ṅán (gzugs ngan) ill-formed, too short in stature S.g.; γzugs ḱúm-pa (gzugs khum pa) to bend, twist one's body, and γzúgs-kyis _otsó-ba (gzugs kyis 'tsho ba), quaestum corpore facere, are given by Sch.; γzugs riṅ-mo (gzugs ring mo) long-stalked Glr. — 3. in physics: body, matter, substance, γzugs-čán (gzugs can), γzugs-su snáṅ-ba (gzugs su snang ba) composed of matter, material, substantial; γzugs-čán ma yín-pa (gzugs can ma yin pa), γzugs-su mi snáṅ-ba (gzugs su mi snang ba), γzugs-méd (gzugs med) immaterial, unsubstantial; γzugs-méd-pai (gzugs med pa'i) (or -kyi (-kyi)) skad (skad) a ghostlike voice Mil.; γzugs-ḱáms (gzugs khams) the range of the material world — γzugs-brnyán (gzugs brnyan) v. brnyan (brnyan).

v. _odzín-pa ('dzin pa); γzuṅ-_odzín (gzung 'dzin) Mil. frq., interest, inclination, bias, γzuṅ-_odzín-brál (gzung 'dzin bral) being free from interest, unbiased, apathetic, which always is praised as an indispensable quality and the true happiness of an ascetic, and the literal equivalent to which in Ssk. may be regarded to be अगृह्य; yet Was. p. 304 renders it
